pipeline.description: 9-step seal pipeline: validate source state, build artifacts, seal envelope, stage for transfer.

steps.name: checkpoint_clean
steps.description: Verify all tracked repos have clean git status (no uncommitted changes). Run Seeds-Checkpoint.ps1 -Validate.

steps.name: tests_pass
steps.description: Run the full test suite for the target project. For GRID-main: make test. For afloat: npm run test. For echoes: uv run pytest tests/ -q --tb=short.

steps.name: lint_pass
steps.description: Run linting for the target project. For GRID-main: make lint. For afloat: npm run lint. For echoes: uv run ruff check .

steps.name: preparedness_gate
steps.description: Check the PreparednessFramework gate for the transfer operation. Gate gate_protocol must return require_approval or log_and_allow. Gate gate_capability must not be blocked without approval.
steps.command: python -c "from boundaries import PreparednessFramework; pf = PreparednessFramework(); print(pf.check_gate('gate_protocol'))"

steps.name: build_artifacts
steps.description: Collect and serialize the artifacts to be transferred. For GRID-main: the payload is a manifest dict with project name, version, commit SHA, file list, and build metadata.

steps.name: seal_envelope
steps.description: Seal the payload into a TransitionEnvelope using seal_envelope(). Computes payload_hash, machine_fingerprint, user_fingerprint, generates nonce, captures timestamp.

steps.name: scope_declaration
steps.description: Attach a ScopeDeclaration to the sealed envelope specifying exactly which permissions the receiver gets. Follow least-privilege: only grant deploy, run_tests, or read_only as needed.
steps.expected_output: Scope declaration embedded in envelope with explicit permissions list.

steps.name: transfer
steps.description: Copy the sealed envelope JSON from E:\Fruits\releases\ to C:\Users\USER\cascadeprojects\gate\incoming\. Use robocopy or Copy-Item with verification.

steps.name: overwatch_log
steps.description: Log the transfer event to Overwatch for monitoring. Emit a gate_transfer_sealed event with envelope_id, timestamp, target partition, and scope summary.
steps.expected_output: Event logged to logs/boundaries/ and Overwatch alert pipeline.

never_rules.rule: Never seal an envelope without running tests first. tests_passed must reflect actual test execution, not a hardcoded True.
Never embed the user_secret in the envelope or write it to any file. The secret is used only transiently for HMAC computation.
Never reuse a nonce across envelopes. Each seal_envelope() call must generate a fresh UUID4 nonce via NonceRegistry.generate().
Never grant scope permissions beyond what the specific transfer requires. Default to read_only; escalate only with explicit justification.
Never transfer unsealed artifacts. The sealed envelope JSON is the ONLY object that may cross the partition boundary.
Never bypass the PreparednessFramework gate. If gate_capability returns ('block', False), the transfer must not proceed.
Never modify the payload after computing payload_hash. Any post-hash mutation invalidates the envelope's integrity proof.
Never skip the checkpoint_clean step. Dirty git state means the source of truth is ambiguous and the envelope cannot be trusted.
Never hardcode the user_secret in source code, config files, or environment variable defaults. Retrieve it from Windows Credential Manager or a secure keyring at runtime only.
Never transfer to a target partition path outside the declared target_partition in the envelope. The receiving path must match the contract.
